meaning: adj.神赐能力的,超凡魅力的
examples: Like all truly charismatic people, he can work his magic on both men and women.
With her striking looks and charismatic personality, she was noticed far and wide.
When the Endangered Species Act was signed in 1973, it was expected to protect charismatic fauna such as the bald eagle and Yellowstone's grizzly bears.
He was a charismatic campaigner, and when elected, a very visible leader who courted publicity.
Hours after the fall, the charismatic politician gave an interview from his hospital bed.
